headline: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
description: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

headline: Macron pays homage to late scholar and Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who joins Panthéon
Panic sweeps across California over proposed billionaire tax
France's ambiguous memory of slavery
description: Although the Code Noir has finally been officially repealed on May 28, it is important to remember that slavery in the French colonies reached its height precisely as the universalist ideals of the Enlightenment were spreading in France, writes journalist Jérôme Gautheret.

headline: North Korea is equipping its navy with nuclear weapons, Kim Jong Un says
description: The North Korean leader made the announcement on Tuesday, during the commissioning of the 'Choe Hyon,' a 5,000-ton warship.

headline: Grieving Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway
description: France head coach Didier Deschamps’ mother died on Tuesday, so he will be returning to France for the funeral, with Guy Stéphan taking over for Friday's final Group I game against Norway. The 2026 World Cup is Deschamps' final tournament in charge of France.

headline: 'A man in shorts is as competent as a man in a suit': Young people push back against office dress codes
description: Some young people push back against traditional dress codes that still prevail in many professional sectors. When temperatures rise, the façade cracks and debates become more heated.

Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
description: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.

headline: The (not so) stupid questions to ask during a heatwave in France
description: Can we swim in the Seine? Should I eat meat when it is 40 degrees? Can I ask for a glass of water in any café? Le Monde answers your questions.

headline: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
description: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.
